key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and functional shape-memory behavior, overcoming key limitations of earlier deposition routes.
engineering_relevance: This enables durable NiTi functional coatings for smart surfaces and components needing actuation, wear resistance, and thermal response.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
Assessed behavior via cooling/loading, 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests
evidence_signal: The coatings showed martensitic transformation and measurable thermal actuation/shape-memory effects despite inhomogeneous microstructure, voids, oxides, dislocations, and internal stress.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings require annealing to realize functional properties, and their tensile strength remains limited.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
